ddls:
CREATE TABLE "weather_records" (
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather record */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the weather station where the data was collected */,
  "timestamp_cst" TEXT /* Local time in CST when the record was taken */,
  "temperature_f" REAL /* Temperature in Fahrenheit */,
  "dew_point_f" REAL /* Dew point in Fahrenheit */,
  "humidity_percent" INTEGER /* Humidity percentage */,
  "sea_level_pressure_in" REAL /* Sea level pressure in inches */,
  "visibility_miles" REAL /* Visibility in miles */,
  "wind_direction" TEXT /* Direction from which the wind is blowing */,
  "wind_speed_mph" REAL /* Wind speed in miles per hour */,
  "gust_speed_mph" REAL /* Gust speed in miles per hour */,
  "precipitation_in" REAL /* Precipitation in inches */,
  "events" TEXT /* Weather events occurring at the time */,
  "conditions" TEXT /* Weather conditions summary */,
  "wind_direction_degrees" INTEGER /* Wind direction in degrees */,
  "timestamp_utc" TEXT /* UTC time when the record was taken */,
  "data_quality_status" TEXT /* Status of data quality check (e.g., passed, failed) */,
  "version" INTEGER /* Version number of the weather record */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_records_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_records_conditions FOREIGN KEY ("conditions") REFERENCES weather_conditions ("condition_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_weather_records_events FOREIGN KEY ("events") REFERENCES weather_events ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_stations" (
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather station */,
  "station_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather station */,
  "location" TEXT /* Location of the weather station */,
  "contact_email" TEXT /* Contact email for inquiries about the station */,
  "latitude" REAL /* Latitude of the weather station */,
  "longitude" REAL /* Longitude of the weather station */,
  "elevation" REAL /* Elevation of the weather station in feet */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_events" (
  "event_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather event */,
  "event_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather event */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the weather event */,
  "severity_level" INTEGER /* Severity level of the weather event (e.g., low, moderate, high) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("event_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "weather_conditions" (
  "condition_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each weather condition */,
  "condition_name" TEXT /* Name of the weather condition */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the weather condition */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the weather condition (e.g., precipitation, wind, temperature) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("condition_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., meteorologist, data analyst, admin) */,
  "password" TEXT /* Password for user authentication */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* ID of the weather record being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the record */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the record was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, download) */,
  "access_location" TEXT /* Location from where the access occurred (e.g., IP address, device ID) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_record_id FOREIGN KEY ("record_id") REFERENCES weather_records ("record_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensors" (
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the weather station where the sensor is installed */,
  "sensor_type" TEXT /* Type of sensor (e.g., temperature, wind speed) */,
  "manufacturer" TEXT /* Manufacturer of the sensor */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date the sensor was installed */,
  "calibration_date" TEXT /* Date the sensor was last calibrated */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("sensor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensors_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "sensor_data" (
  "data_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each sensor data entry */,
  "sensor_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the sensor that collected the data */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the weather record that includes this sensor data */,
  "measurement" REAL /* The actual measurement taken by the sensor */,
  "measurement_time" TEXT /* Time when the measurement was taken */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("data_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_data_sensor_id FOREIGN KEY ("sensor_id") REFERENCES sensors ("sensor_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_sensor_data_record_id FOREIGN KEY ("record_id") REFERENCES weather_records ("record_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "station_maintenance" (
  "maintenance_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each maintenance event */,
  "station_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the weather station that was maintained */,
  "maintenance_date" TEXT /* Date when the maintenance occurred */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the maintenance performed */,
  "technician_id" INTEGER /* ID of the technician who performed the maintenance */,
  "cost" REAL /* Cost of the maintenance */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("maintenance_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_station_maintenance_technician_id FOREIGN KEY ("technician_id") REFERENCES technicians ("technician_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_station_maintenance_station_id FOREIGN KEY ("station_id") REFERENCES weather_stations ("station_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "technicians" (
  "technician_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each technician */,
  "technician_name" TEXT /* Full name of the technician */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the technician */,
  "certification" TEXT /* Certification held by the technician */,
  "expiration_date" TEXT /* Expiration date of the technician's certification */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("technician_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_reports" (
  "report_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report */,
  "report_name" TEXT /* Name of the report */,
  "start_date" TEXT /* Start date for the period covered by the report */,
  "end_date" TEXT /* End date for the period covered by the report */,
  "summary" TEXT /* Summary of the report's findings */,
  "author_id" INTEGER /* ID of the author who generated the report */,
  "report_type" TEXT /* Type of report (e.g., monthly, seasonal, annual) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("report_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_reports_author_id FOREIGN KEY ("author_id") REFERENCES report_authors ("author_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "report_authors" (
  "author_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each report author */,
  "author_name" TEXT /* Full name of the author */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the author */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the author belongs to */,
  "expertise" TEXT /* Area of expertise of the author */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("author_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "data_quality" (
  "quality_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each quality check */,
  "record_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the weather record that was checked */,
  "check_date" TEXT /* Date when the quality check was performed */,
  "status" TEXT /* Status of the quality check (e.g., passed, failed) */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes on the quality check results */,
  "checked_by" INTEGER /* ID of the person who performed the quality check */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("quality_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_quality_record_id FOREIGN KEY ("record_id") REFERENCES weather_records ("record_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_data_quality_checked_by FOREIGN KEY ("checked_by") REFERENCES quality_checkers ("checker_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "quality_checkers" (
  "checker_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each quality checker */,
  "checker_name" TEXT /* Full name of the quality checker */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the quality checker */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the quality checker belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("checker_id")
)
